lesson: Forward all caller args untouched with def wrapper(func, *args, **kwargs): return func(*args, **kwargs) to wrap any-signature callables.
Never use a mutable literal (list/dict/set) as a default arg; use default=None and create the mutable inside the function body.
Fix late-binding closures in a loop by defaulting the loop var (lambda i=i: ...) or wrapping in a factory function to capture it by value.
Use functools.partial(func, *fixed_args, **fixed_kwargs) to freeze some parameters and get a specialized callable without a lambda or def.
Build parameterized decorators as decorator(func)-returning outer functions, and apply functools.wraps(func) on the inner wrapper to preserve __name__/__doc__.
Implement right-to-left function composition with functools.reduce(lambda acc,f: f(acc), reversed(funcs), x); zero funcs must reduce to identity.
Support @deco and @deco(kw=v) on one function by giving the outer function a _func=None first positional param and dispatching on whether it is callable.
Key a memoizing closure with (args, frozenset(kwargs.items())) to hash keyword calls, and expose mutable closure state (hit/miss stats) via functions assigned as wrapper attributes.
